tagline: Turn daily moments into instant learning.

designSystem.style: Warm earth tones with ivory background, terracotta accents, forest green highlights, and organic soft shadows. Clean card-based layout with rounded corners and gentle gradients.

screens: Home — Context-aware lesson feed with streak card, location banner, and 4 categorized micro-lesson cards (Language, Finance, Health, Work) that open into a 3-step lesson modal
Scan — Camera scan interface with sample receipt, menu, and label items that decode unfamiliar terms using AI-style explanations with a lesson CTA
Skills Map — 2-column grid of 6 life-area skill cards (Money, Travel, Health, Work, Language, Food) with progress bars, levels, and recent achievement badges
Profile — User settings with toggle controls for context-aware triggers, notifications, and dark mode, plus focus area management and app preferences
description: Lesson Lens is an AI-powered microlearning app that helps people learn practical knowledge by transforming everyday experiences into short, personalized lessons. Instead of expecting users to sit down for long study sessions, it watches for contextual moments like a commute, a grocery trip, or a confusing work task and delivers a tiny lesson exactly when it is most useful. The app solves the common problem that people forget most of what they try to learn because it is disconnected from real life and delivered at the wrong time. A user could scan a restaurant menu while traveling and get a 90-second language lesson on ordering politely, or receive a quick finance lesson after seeing a confusing payroll deduction on a pay stub. What makes it unique is that it uses the user's surroundings, calendar, and recent activity to teach only what is immediately relevant, turning routine moments into memorable, applied education. Over time, Lesson Lens builds a personal
features: Context-aware lesson triggers detect situations like a commute, shopping trip, or work task and surface a lesson that matches the moment, which makes learning feel useful instead of abstract.
Micro-lessons are delivered in 60 to 90 seconds with one actionable takeaway, so users can learn even when they are busy and still apply the knowledge right away.
The app can read receipts, menus, forms, and labels to explain unfamiliar terms in plain language, helping users understand the world around them without leaving the app.
Adaptive review reminders revisit lessons only when the user is likely to need them again, which improves retention without overwhelming them with unnecessary notifications.
A personal skill map tracks what the user has learned across life areas like money, travel, health, and work, helping them see progress and spot gaps in their everyday knowledge.
audience: Adults ages 18-45 who want practical learning that fits into busy schedules, including commuters, first-job professionals, travelers, and parents juggling limited free time. It is especially useful for people who prefer real-world application over traditional courses.
useCases: Sarah just finished a stressful meeting and notices a contract term she does not understand, so she opens Lesson Lens to get a plain-English explanation and a quick lesson on what to ask next.
Marcus is traveling in a country where he does not speak the language, and when he points his camera at a café menu, the app teaches him the exact phrases he needs to order confidently.
Alyssa is shopping for her first apartment and gets overwhelmed by insurance jargon, so she uses the app to turn the lease and utility forms into short lessons she can understand before signing.

designSystem.style: Sunset gradient with warm white base, amber-to-plum gradient accents, soft pink highlights, rounded corners throughout, glassmorphic cards, Dynamic Island notch, smooth fade-in transitions and micro-interaction button press effects

screens: Home/Explore — Browse nearby trade listings with category filters, eco-points banner, QR quick-access, item cards with like/message actions
Trade Hub — Post new items with category picker and quantity selector, QR code quick-trade modal, waste category tracking with monthly stats and trade history
Eco-Points — Points balance card with level progress, shop redemption list with affordability check, earned/spent stats breakdown
Community — Local trader stats, upcoming events with RSVP and eco-point rewards, eco tips and sustainability advice
Profile — User stats (trades, points, kg saved), badge collection grid with locked/earned states, settings menu
description: Trash Trader connects people who want to get rid of unused items with those looking for sustainable solutions. Users can trade things like empty glass jars, fabric scraps, or other waste materials for eco-points redeemable at local sustainable shops and businesses.
features: QR code scanning for quick trades

tagline: See your habits before they cost you.

designSystem.style: Soft pastel airy minimal — light cream background with muted rose, sage green, and lavender accents. Rounded cards, gradient hero panels, micro-interaction toggles, and animated bar charts. Professional hierarchy with bold numeric callouts and subdued supporting text.

screens: Home — Dashboard with weekly spend summary card (gradient), trigger moment chips, and scrollable transaction timeline with trigger badges
Patterns — Interactive pattern replay with tabbed trigger selector, animated daily bar chart, related transactions list, and SpendEcho insight cards
Predict — Impulse forecast with model accuracy meter, expandable risk window cards with confidence bars, intervention nudges, and past prediction history
Goals — Habit-based savings goals with animated toggle switches, progress bars, total saved summary, and low-cost ritual suggestions
description: SpendEcho turns bank transactions into a timeline of recurring life patterns, spotting hidden “trigger moments” like stress-shopping Fridays, post-payday splurges, or commute-linked spending. It then predicts the next impulse window and offers tiny, context-aware interventions such as cooling-off goals, stash transfers, or alternative low-cost rituals.
audience: People trying to break subtle overspending habits

tagline: Train by how your body feels.

designSystem.style: Monochrome warm — soft pastel palette with charcoal typography, warm accent orange, and semantic color coding (green=peak, yellow=good, orange=moderate, red=rest). Clean card-based layouts with subtle borders and rounded corners. Typographic hierarchy with uppercase tracking labels.

screens: Pulse (Home) — Body readiness score with animated donut ring, health signal cards (sleep/HRV/soreness/stress), and personalized daily workout recommendation
Move (Workout) — Mood selector grid (Energized/Calm/Stressed/Tired) that dynamically filters suggested workout sessions with expandable detail cards and session start
Recovery — Hydration tracker with tap-to-fill glass UI, recovery metric grid, and checklist-style stretching routine
Trends — Interactive 7-day bar chart for HRV/Sleep/Resting HR, connected data source toggles, and AI pattern insights

components.dynamicIsland: Black pill with camera/sensor indicators at top of phone
components.statusBar: Time + wifi + battery in DM Mono
components.bottomNav: 4-tab navigation with filled square icon backgrounds for active state
components.readinessScore: Large numeric score with SVG donut ring, semantic color badge, and contextual description
components.signalCards: 2-column grid with icon, value, and thin progress bar
components.moodSelector: 2x2 emoji grid with border highlight on selection
components.workoutCards: Expandable cards showing workout phases and start CTA
components.barChart: Custom SVG-free bar chart with per-day labels and active highlight
components.toggleRow: iOS-style pill toggle with smooth thumb animation

description: Pulse Palette turns messy daily health signals into a simple color map that tells users what kind of workout their body is ready for. It combines wearable data, sleep, mood, soreness, hydration, and cycle tracking to recommend personalized micro-sessions, recovery plans, or rest days in a way that feels intuitive instead of data-heavy.
audience: Busy adults who want smarter, safer fitness

tagline: Turn downtime into interactive story episodes.

designSystem.style: Nordic minimal — generous whitespace, clean card layouts, soft pastel accents on a near-white base. Subtle shadows and hairline borders define structure without visual noise. Typography-first hierarchy with slate blue as the primary interactive color.

screens: Home — Mood picker (5 emotional states), horizontal continue-listening carousel, full story card list with genre/rating/listener metadata and inline progress bars

Now Playing — Hero cover art panel, playback progress bar with play/pause, Smart Recap card summarizing previous episodes, interactive branching choice cards that unlock at 72% progress with animated result reveal
Discover — Full-text search input, horizontal genre filter pills, Editor's Pick featured card, filtered story list responding to search and genre selection
Library — Three-tab view (Saved / In Progress / Finished), offline availability banner, compact story cards with bookmark toggle
Profile — User stats grid (episodes, hours, choices, streak), genre preference chips, settings panel with animated toggles (notifications, offline, autoplay), recent listening history list
description: PlotPulse turns idle moments like commutes, waiting rooms, and chores into personalized audio drama episodes that adapt to your mood, time, and interests. Instead of endlessly scrolling for something to watch or read, the app delivers bite-sized interactive stories where your choices shape the next scene and each session picks up where you left off.

designSystem.style: Citrus fresh — energetic, bold, modern UI with bright orange primary, lemon yellow accents, and lime green trust indicators. White background with soft pastel tint surfaces. Heavy use of rounded cards, gradient CTAs, and emoji iconography for warmth and approachability.

screens: Home/Nearby — Live feed of hyperlocal micro-requests sorted by urgency and distance, with category filter chips, quick-post banner, and like/save interactions
Request — Step-by-step request creation with category grid, title/description inputs, time-window selector (Now/1hr/Today), and anonymous location info card
Matches — Active chat interface with anonymous-first messaging, past connections with star ratings, and trust stats dashboard (helped, requested, trust score)
Profile — User identity card with trust score breakdown, earned badges grid, skills/items offered, and live availability toggle
description: Neighbor Loop helps people solve small, urgent daily problems by connecting them with trusted nearby residents who can lend a tool, share a ride, watch a package, or give quick local advice. Instead of public posts or broad groups, the app matches users through micro-requests based on time, distance, and skills, creating practical neighborhood connections without awkward searching.
audience: Urban and suburban neighbors who need quick local help

designSystem.style: Citrus fresh, energetic, bold, modern. White backgrounds with vibrant orange, yellow, and mint green accents. Heavy typography with rounded card components and gradient hero sections.

screens: Home — Dashboard with mood score hero card (orange gradient), weekly mood bar chart, workout streak stats, recent workout history with before/after mood deltas, and AI-powered workout suggestion
Workout — Live workout tracker with real-time heart rate + calorie counter and timer, category filter chips, workout cards with mood boost ratings and expandable start flow
Trends — Emotional trend analysis with before/after bar charts, avg mood boost headline stat, per-activity mood impact bar rankings, and AI-generated personalized insights
Community — Active challenges with join/progress tracking, community feed with mood-tagged workout posts, likes and replies, participant counts and countdowns
Profile — User profile with stats summary, achievement badges, wearable device connection status, and preference toggles for notifications and auto mood detection
description: MoodFit Monitor tracks your emotional responses during workouts, helping you identify the mental benefits of different exercises. By syncing with wearables, it provides personalized feedback on how activities affect your mood, guiding you to choose workouts that enhance both fitness and mental health.

tagline: Your library of soundscapes for every mood.

designSystem.style: Sunset gradient with warm pastel palette — amber, soft pink, and deep plum accents. Rounded cards, gradient art blocks, glassmorphism Dynamic Island, smooth micro-interactions, and layered shadows for depth.

screens: Home/Discover — Mood selector grid with 6 emotional states, AI soundscape generator with animated loading, featured horizontal scroll cards, and trending list with play controls
Player — Full-screen soundscape player with animated art, waveform visualizer, scrubber with live progress, volume slider, playback controls, mood tag, and share action
Library — Personal soundscape collection with playlist shortcuts, mood filter chips, and saved echo cards with bookmark/save toggles
Social/Community — Creator spotlight carousel with follow toggles, community posts with soundscape previews, like/share/reply actions, and hashtag discovery
Profile — User stats banner, Apple Watch wearable integration toggle with biometrics display, AI preference sliders, and settings panel
description: Mood Echo curates personalized soundscapes based on your emotional state, using AI-driven algorithms to analyze your preferences and vibes. Simply input how you're feeling, and the app generates a unique audio experience combining ambient sound, music, and nature sounds to enhance your mood and provide a tranquil escape.

designSystem.style: Warm earth tones with ivory background, terracotta highlights, forest green accents, soft shadows, organic rounded shapes, card-based layout with sand borders

screens: Home — Dashboard with streak stats, accuracy bar, scan CTA, and recent mistake feed with severity indicators
Scanner — Camera viewfinder with grid overlay, upload options, scanning/analyzing/results flow with found errors and concept gaps
Atlas — Dark-themed misconception heatmap with glowing concept nodes, subject filter chips, prerequisite gap list with intensity bars
Quests — Remediation quest cards with XP rewards, progress bars, due timers; interactive step-by-step exercise modal with reveal answers
Profile — Student avatar with stats header, subject progress breakdown, achievements grid, settings rows
description: Mistake Atlas helps students turn wrong answers into personalized learning maps. By scanning homework, quizzes, or practice problems, the app identifies the exact misconception behind each error and generates a visual trail of prerequisite gaps to fix first. Instead of reviewing entire chapters, learners get a targeted path that rebuilds understanding from the root.
audience: Middle school to college students who struggle with repeated mistakes

designSystem.style: Sunset gradient with warm ivory background, amber and soft pink gradients, deep plum primary. Rounded cards with subtle drop shadows, gradient accents on CTAs and highlights. Clean hierarchy with Sora typeface across all weights.

screens: Home — Dashboard with live session banner, quick stats (sessions/challenges/connections), horizontally scrollable mentor cards, and active challenge progress
Sessions — Live session browser with topic filters, active session controls (mute/raise hand/leave), session cards with RSVP, and a community Q&A input
Mentors — Expert Q&A feed with like interactions, searchable mentor directory with availability status, tag chips, follow and book-session actions
Pathway — Real-world challenge cards (expandable with descriptions and start CTA), career track progress bars with step-by-step milestone visualization for UX/Data/Product paths
description: Future Focus connects students with industry professionals for live virtual mentorship sessions. Users can engage in Q&A, receive personalized advice on career paths, and access real-time project challenges. This interactive platform fosters valuable connections, guiding students from academic knowledge to practical application.

designSystem.style: Sunset gradient with warm pastel palette — amber, soft pink, and deep plum. Rounded cards, gradient accents, and smooth micro-interactions. Clean typography hierarchy using Sora for headings and Quicksand for body text.

screens: Home — Dashboard with AI focus score, daily stats (deep work, breaks, tasks), AI behavior insights, and top distractions bar chart
Focus — Technique selector (Pomodoro, Deep Work, Quick Burst, Flow State), animated SVG ring timer with gradient stroke, play/pause/reset/break controls, current task display with tags
Analytics — Weekly summary cards, deep work bar chart, hourly focus intensity chart, AI behavior report with personalized pattern insights

description: FocusPal uses AI to analyze your behavior patterns and environments to identify common distractions. It then recommends personalized focus techniques and schedules to boost productivity throughout your day. Enhance your workflow with smart reminders, music playlists, and analytics designed just for you.

designSystem.style: Candy pop — playful bubbly design with soft pastel backgrounds, electric violet and hot pink accents, mint highlights, rounded corners, and micro-interaction press effects

screens: Home — Featured leftover recipe cards, meal category grid (Breakfast/Lunch/Dinner/Snacks), daily pro tip banner, and gradient header with greeting
Analyzer — Ingredient input with quick-add chips, ingredient tag management, animated analyze button with loading state, match results with zero-waste score, flavor enhancer suggestions

description: Flavor Switch helps home cooks reinvent their leftovers into exciting, restaurant-quality dishes. By inputting what ingredients you have on hand, the app generates unique recipes, cooking tips, and complementary flavor profiles to enhance your meal experience. Say goodbye to wasting food and hello to culinary creativity!

tagline: Train your mind and body together.

designSystem.style: Sunset gradient with warm seashell background, amber and soft pink accents, deep plum for hierarchy. Rounded corners throughout, gradient card headers, glassmorphism overlays on hero sections.

screens: Home — Mood check-in grid, AI workout recommendation card, wellness score progress bar, today's schedule, and key stats (streak, workouts, mindful minutes)
Workout — Adaptive workout detail with live timer, exercise checklist with completion toggles, progress bar, and intensity/duration metadata

Community — Support group cards with join toggle, social feed with like/comment interactions, member counts and post activity
description: Emotion Fit is a revolutionary app that integrates emotional well-being with physical fitness by using AI to tailor workouts based on your mood. Users can engage in dynamic workouts that adapt in real-time, providing support for mental states while improving fitness levels. Experience a personalized blend of mindfulness and exercise every time you work out.

description: EcoSwap connects local communities through a platform for trading unwanted items. Users can list items they wish to give away or swap, promoting recycling and reducing waste in creative ways. With filters for categories and exchange suggestions, finding the perfect item or home for your belongings has never been easier.

tagline: Turn travel delays into mini adventures.

designSystem.style: Warm earth tones with organic shapes, soft shadows, and terracotta accents on an ivory base. Features gradient headers, timeline-based layouts, and tactile card components.

screens: Home — Live flight delay dashboard with energy/budget selectors and one-tap plan generation CTA
Explore — Filterable experience cards with time, distance, cost, and weather context
Plan — Generated drift timeline with scored itinerary, stops, and share/redo actions
Saved — Favorited experiences and past drift trip history with personal stats
description: Delay Drift helps travelers salvage disrupted itineraries by instantly generating nearby, time-safe experiences matched to their exact layover, weather, budget, and energy level. It scans real-time transit changes and curates hyperlocal options like express museum loops, snack trails, skyline walks, and one-hour neighborhood missions that fit the gap perfectly.

designSystem.style: Sunset gradient with warm pastel palette — warm white background, amber and soft pink accents, deep plum as primary brand color. Rounded corners throughout, gradient pill buttons, card-based layout with subtle borders.

screens: Timeline — 30-day forward-looking cash-flow chart with interactive bar visualization, day-by-day balance list, payday markers, and stress alerts
Bills — Recurring charge detector with categorized bill list, defer suggestion feature, auto-detected subscription badges, and monthly total summary
Alerts — Smart spending intelligence hub with safe-to-spend card, dismissable alert cards for low balance warnings, payday countdowns, and irregular expense tips
Simulate — What-if expense calculator with real-time impact analysis, quick amount shortcuts, common scenario presets, and post-payday recommendations
description: BillEcho turns recurring bills, subscriptions, and expected purchases into a forward-looking cash-flow timeline, so users can spot money stress before it hits. It learns paydays, spending patterns, and irregular expenses to forecast safe-to-spend amounts in a highly visual, day-by-day view.
audience: Workers and freelancers with irregular cash flow

designSystem.style: Zen garden — calm, spa-like aesthetic with off-white backgrounds, matcha green accents, warm gray tones, and soft gold highlights. Rounded corners, subtle borders, and gentle gradients create a serene, balanced atmosphere.

screens: Discover — Home feed with trending pairings, featured AI-curated combos, seasonal highlights, and a quick ingredient search bar
Match — Ingredient input workspace with dynamic flavor profile bars (umami, acidity, fat, herbal), quick-add suggestions, and AI analysis trigger
Match Results — AI-generated recipe suggestions with flavor match scores, cooking time, difficulty, flavor notes, side dish recommendations, and curated drink pairings
Recipes — Saved recipe collection with category filters (All, Quick, Vegetarian, Seafood, Dessert), star ratings, and AI recipe generator CTA
Community — Social feed with trending/recent/following tabs, pairing posts with combination highlights, tags, likes, comments, and save-to-try actions
Profile — User taste personality card, flavor preferences, saved combinations list, stats (pairings/saved/shared), and settings
description: Flavor Match uses AI to analyze individual ingredients and suggests complementary dishes or pairings that bring out their best flavors. By inputting what you have on hand or what you're craving, the app generates tailored recipes and suggests complementary sides and drinks, enhancing your dining experience.
features: Pairing suggestions for sides and drinks
Community feature for sharing favorite combinations

description: TripSync is your travel companion that helps families seamlessly coordinate and match their travel experiences. By pooling recommendations, personal schedules, and itineraries, family members can collaborate in real-time to enrich their ongoing trip and create unforgettable memories together.

designSystem.style: Dark fantasy RPG aesthetic with vibrant purple and gold accents. Deep navy background with glowing card surfaces, gradient quest banners, and gamified UI elements. Rounded corners, emoji-rich iconography, and XP-driven progress indicators throughout.

screens: Map — Quest board with active quest banner, XP level progress bar, available quests list with difficulty/subject tags, and daily challenges tracker
Quest — Immersive storytelling screen with gradient scene header, NPC dialogue bubble, knowledge challenge question, interactive multiple-choice answers with correct/incorrect feedback and XP reward
Hero — Character profile with customizable avatar color aura, level and class display, four character stats with progress bars, and 2x3 achievement badge grid
League — Weekly leaderboard with 3-player podium visualization, full ranked scholar list with streaks, and open Study Party invitation with join CTA
Progress — Analytics dashboard with stats summary row, weekly XP bar chart, subject mastery progress bars, and earned/locked achievement badge gallery
description: QuestEd transforms the traditional learning experience into an immersive adventure game. Users engage with educational content by embarking on quests that challenge their knowledge and skills, earning rewards as they progress. The app covers various subjects while fostering creativity and critical thinking in a fun and engaging way.
